# Team News: Scotland vs. Morocco injury, suspension list, predicted XIs

By Matt Law| 18 Jun 2026 22:00, Last updated: 18 Jun 2026 22:00

© Imago / Loulidiphoto

Scotland and Morocco will continue their respective 2026 World Cup campaigns with a clash on Friday.

Group C is currently topped by Scotland with three points, while Morocco are second on one point, and here Sports Mole rounds up the team news for the game.

---

## SCOTLAND VS. MOROCCO

https://www.sportsmole.co.uk/football/scotland/world-cup-2026/preview/scotland-vs-morocco-prediction-team-news-lineups_599454.html

## SCOTLAND

Out: None

Doubtful: Scott McKenna(calf)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Gunn; Hickey, Hendry, Hanley, Robertson; Gannon-Doak, Ferguson, McTominay, McGinn; Adams, Shankland

## MOROCCO

Out: None

Doubtful: None

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Bono; Hakimi, Diop, Ri

news_items.title: Scotland XI vs Morocco: Predicted lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today - Yahoo Sports
news_items.author: Oli Gent Fri, 19 June 2026 at 11:07 am UTC · 2 min read
news_items.text:
Scotland XI vs Morocco: Predicted lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today - Yahoo Sports

Advertisement

Advertisement

Advertisement

Advertisement

Story by

Scotland XI vs Morocco: Predicted lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today

Scotland have one injury concern going into their huge World Cup encounter with Morocco today.

Steve Clarke is likely to be without defender Scott McKenna once more, with the former Nottingham Forest man nursing a calf injury that kept him out of the 1-0 win over Haiti.

Advertisement

Advertisement

Otherwise, the Scots are at full strength, and it remains to be seen whether Clarke goes for the jugular against the slick Moroccans, or whether he reverts to type and hopes to grind his way to avoiding defeat.

He has switched to a more adventurous 4-4-2 shape in recent weeks and months, incorporating the prolific Lawrence Shankland into his attack to partner Che Adams.

That was the strike partnership against Haiti, and Lyndon Dykes was introduced with 15 minutes to play to add more aerial threat.

Advertisement

Advertisement

We could see the Charlton target man deployed here ahead of either Shankland or

# Scotland vs. Morocco projected lineups, starting 11 for World Cup 2026 Group C game at Gillette Stadium

Marios Hamboullas

•

SN

Add The Sporting News on Google

Scotland and Morocco meet on Friday at Gillette Stadium in a World Cup Group C game that could prove pivotal in the race to reach the knockout phase.

Scotland began their campaign with a narrow 1-0 win over Haiti, while African champions Morocco earned a creditable 1-1 draw against Brazil on matchday one. That has left both sides well placed in a competitive group,

news_items.title: Scotland get defensive injury boost as Steve Clarke’s side prepare for Morocco | STV News
news_items.author: PA Sport
news_items.text:
Scotland get defensive injury boost as Steve Clarke’s side prepare for Morocco | STV News

The 29-year-old Dinamo Zagreb centre-back missed the 1-0 Group C win over Haiti on Saturday with a calf injury

##### Posted in

Last updated Jun 16th, 2026 at 17:20

Scott McKenna returned to training ahead of Scotland’s World Cup clash with Morocco on Friday night.

The 29-year-old Dinamo Zagreb centre-back missed the 1-0 Group C win over Haiti on Saturday with a calf injury which has curtailed his preparations.

However, boss Steve Clarke now has a full complement of players for their second group game at the Boston stadium.

With Morocco and Brazil drawing 1-1 in their group opener, the Scots are top of the table with three points as they look to qualify for the knockout stages for the first time.

After taking on Morocco, Scotland, who are in the World Cup for the first time since 1998, fly to Miami for their final Group C fixture against the Brazilians on June 24.

##### STV News is now on WhatsApp

Get all the latest news from around the country

# Team News: Scotland vs. Morocco injury, suspension list, predicted XIs

By Matt Law| 18 Jun 2026 22:00, Last updated: 18 Jun 2026 22:00

© Imago / Loulidiphoto

Scotland and Morocco will continue their respective 2026 World Cup campaigns with a clash on Friday.

Group C is currently topped by Scotland with three points, while Morocco are second on one point, and here Sports Mole rounds up the team news for the game.

---

## SCOTLAND VS. MOROCCO

https://www.sportsmole.co.uk/football/scotland/world-cup-2026/preview/scotland-vs-morocco-prediction-team-news-lineups_599454.html

## MOROCCO

Out: None

Scotland XI vs Morocco: Predicted lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today

Scotland have one injury concern going into their huge World Cup encounter with Morocco today.

Steve Clarke is likely to be without defender Scott McKenna once more, with the former Nottingham Forest man nursing a calf injury that kept him out of the 1-0 win over Haiti.

Advertisement

Advertisement

Otherwise, the Scots are at full strength, and it remains to be seen whether Clarke goes for the jugular against the slick Moroccans, or whether he reverts to type and hopes to grind his way to avoiding defeat.

He has switched to a more adventurous 4-4-2 shape in recent weeks and months, incorporating the prolific Lawrence Shankland into his attack to partner Che Adams.

That was the strike partnership against Haiti, and Lyndon Dykes was introduced with 15 minutes to play to add more aerial threat.

Marios Hamboullas

•

SN

Add The Sporting News on Google

Scotland and Morocco meet on Friday at Gillette Stadium in a World Cup Group C game that could prove pivotal in the race to reach the knockout phase.

Scotland began their campaign with a narrow 1-0 win over Haiti, while African champions Morocco earned a creditable 1-1 draw against Brazil on matchday one. That has left both sides well placed in a competitive group,"}

{"title": "Scotland get defensive injury boost as Steve Clarke’s side prepare for Morocco | STV News", "url": "https://news.stv.tv/sport/scotland-get-defensive-injury-boost-as-steve-clarkes-side-prepare-for-morocco", "publishedDate": "2026-06-16T15:39:40.000Z", "author": "PA Sport", "text": "Scotland get defensive injury boost as Steve Clarke’s side prepare for Morocco | STV News

The 29-year-old Dinamo Zagreb centre-back missed the 1-0 Group C win over Haiti on Saturday with a calf injury

##### Posted in

Last updated Jun 16th, 2026 at 17:20

Scott McKenna returned to training ahead of Scotland’s World Cup clash with Morocco on Friday night.

The 29-year-old Dinamo Zagreb centre-back missed the 1-0 Group C win over Haiti on Saturday with a calf injury which has curtailed his preparations.

However, boss Steve Clarke now has a full complement of players for their second group game at the Boston stadium.

With Morocco and Brazil drawing 1-1 in their group opener, the Scots are top of the table with three points as they look to qualify for the knockout stages for the first time.

After taking on Morocco, Scotland, who are in the World Cup for the first time since 1998, fly to Miami for their final Group C fixture against the Brazilians on June 24.
